capstone-domain-model.label: Capstone Domain Model
capstone-domain-model.summary: Build the task objects that carry the tracker domain before adding storage or reports.

project.title: Smart Task Tracker Domain Model Capstone

capstone_domain_model_step2_feature_task.title: Add the FeatureTask subclass with validation

capstone_domain_model_step4_package_runn.title: Organize the models package and runner

try_capstone_domain_model_sketch0.title: Create the shared BaseTask model
try_capstone_domain_model_sketch0.prompt: In `models/base_task.py`, implement `BaseTask` with `title`, validated `priority`, and `completed` state. New tasks start incomplete. `mark_done()` sets completed to True, `is_open()` returns the opposite, and `summary()` raises NotImplementedError. The supplied main.py marks one task done and prints its state.
try_capstone_domain_model_sketch0.hint: Validate before storing the fields, then mutate only `self.completed` in mark_done().

help.concept: The base class owns shared state and behavior while leaving the concrete summary format to subclasses.
help.hint_1: Validate before storing the fields, then mutate only `self.completed` in mark_done().
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

try_capstone_domain_model_sketch0.starterCode:
# Run this provided BaseTask state demo.
from models.base_task import BaseTask

task = BaseTask("Draft roadmap", "high")
task.mark_done()
print(task.title)
print(task.priority)
print(task.completed)

main_py.content:

task.mark_done()
print(task.title)
print(task.priority)
print(task.completed)

print(task.priority)
print(task.completed)

try_capstone_domain_model_sketch0.solutionCode:
from models.base_task import BaseTask

task = BaseTask("Draft roadmap", "high")
task.mark_done()
print(task.title)
print(task.priority)
print(task.completed)

1.message: A marked-done task should no longer be open.

2.message: Print the title, priority, and final completed state.

capstone_domain_model_step1_bug_task.title: Add the BugTask subclass
capstone_domain_model_step1_bug_task.prompt: Implement `BugTask` in `models/bug_task.py`. Inherit from BaseTask, use super() for shared fields, store severity, and return `BUG: <title> | priority=<priority> | severity=<severity> | done=<completed>` from summary().

help.concept: Inheritance reuses shared state while overriding the behavior that differs.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

capstone_domain_model_step1_bug_task.starterCode:
# Run this provided before-and-after demo.
from models.bug_task import BugTask

task = BugTask("Fix login redirect", "high", "critical")
print(task.summary())
task.mark_done()
print(task.summary())

main_py.content:
# Run this provided before-and-after demo.

task.mark_done()
print(task.summary())

from models.bug_task import BugTask

capstone_domain_model_step1_bug_task.solutionCode:

capstone_domain_model_step1_bug_task.expectedOutput:
BUG: Fix login redirect | priority=high | severity=critical | done=False
BUG: Fix login redirect | priority=high | severity=critical | done=True

capstone_domain_model_step2_feature_task.title: Add FeatureTask with validation
capstone_domain_model_step2_feature_task.prompt: Implement `FeatureTask` in `models/feature_task.py`. Inherit from BaseTask, clamp estimate_points to at least 1, and return `FEATURE: <title> | priority=<priority> | points=<estimate_points> | done=<completed>`.
capstone_domain_model_step2_feature_task.hint: Use max(1, int(estimate_points)) after calling super().

help.concept: A subclass can add its own validated state while keeping the shared lifecycle behavior.
help.hint_1: Use max(1, int(estimate_points)) after calling super().
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

capstone_domain_model_step3_task_service.title: Create a polymorphic task service
capstone_domain_model_step3_task_service.prompt: Complete `build_report_lines(tasks)` in `services/task_service.py`. Return each task.summary() result in order. The supplied main.py passes a mixed BugTask and FeatureTask list.
capstone_domain_model_step3_task_service.hint: Use one expression for every task; do not branch by concrete class.

help.concept: Polymorphism lets a service work with the shared summary() contract.
help.hint_1: Use one expression for every task; do not branch by concrete class.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

capstone_domain_model_step3_task_service.starterCode:
# Run this provided mixed-task demo.
from models import BugTask, FeatureTask
from services.task_service import build_report_lines

tasks = [
    BugTask("Fix login redirect", "high", "critical"),
    FeatureTask("Add dark mode", "medium", 3),
]
for line in build_report_lines(tasks):
    print(line)

main_py.content:

from services.task_service import build_report_lines

tasks = [

capstone_domain_model_step4_package_runn.prompt: Complete `models/__init__.py` so main.py can import BaseTask, BugTask, and FeatureTask directly from models. Keep the three-task demo and mark only the bug task done.
capstone_domain_model_step4_package_runn.hint: Import each class in models/__init__.py and define __all__.

help.concept: A package entry point keeps imports clean without copying class definitions into main.py.
help.hint_1: Import each class in models/__init__.py and define __all__.

    FeatureTask("Add dark mode", "medium", 3),
    FeatureTask("Export CSV", "low", 5),
]
tasks[0].mark_done()

    print(line)

capstone_domain_model_step5_final_domain.title: Finish the domain-model handoff
capstone_domain_model_step5_final_domain.prompt: Complete `count_open_tasks(tasks)` in `services/task_service.py`. Count tasks through their inherited is_open() behavior. The final demo prints four summaries and `Open tasks: 2`.
capstone_domain_model_step5_final_domain.hint: Sum the boolean result of task.is_open() for every task.

help.concept: The final domain layer combines encapsulation, inheritance, overriding, and polymorphism without duplicate classes.
help.hint_1: Sum the boolean result of task.is_open() for every task.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

capstone_domain_model_step5_final_domain.starterCode:
# Run this provided final domain demo.
from models import BugTask, FeatureTask
from services.task_service import build_report_lines, count_open_tasks

tasks = [
    BugTask("Fix login redirect", "high", "critical"),
    BugTask("Resolve crash on save", "high", "major"),
    FeatureTask("Add dark mode", "medium", 3),
    FeatureTask("Export CSV", "low", 5),
]
tasks[0].mark_done()
tasks[3].mark_done()
for line in build_report_lines(tasks):
    print(line)
print(f"Open tasks: {count_open_tasks(tasks)}")

main_py.content:

]
tasks[0].mark_done()
tasks[3].mark_done()
for line in build_report_lines(tasks):
    print(line)
print(f"Open tasks: {count_open_tasks(tasks)}")

capstone_domain_model_step5_final_domain.expectedOutput:
BUG: Fix login redirect | priority=high | severity=critical | done=True
BUG: Resolve crash on save | priority=high | severity=major | done=False
FEATURE: Add dark mode | priority=medium | points=3 | done=False
FEATURE: Export CSV | priority=low | points=5 | done=True
Open tasks: 2

capstone-domain-model-sketch-0.title: Capstone domain model: make the objects carry the app idea
capstone-domain-model-sketch-0.bodyMarkdown:
A capstone app needs domain objects that mean something.

In a recipe planner, you might have a base `RecipeItem` and specialized items like `MealPrepTask` or `ShoppingTask`. Each object stores its own state and knows how to summarize itself.

The Smart Task Tracker applies the same idea to product-team work. A task has a title, status, points, and a summary. Subclasses can represent different kinds of work while sharing the same interface.

Build the model slowly: base class first, subclasses second, service layer only after the objects are clear.
